query:
The profiler is set up with Approach A. The app added @nestjs/graphql + @nestjs/apollo + graphql with GraphQLModule.forRoot() (context not customized).

User: Add the GraphQL collector.
expected_behavior: Must read references/collectors.md. Register ProfilerGraphQLModule.forRoot() alongside GraphQLModule, gated the same way as the core. Apply the gotcha: the GraphQLModule context must expose the request — for Apollo, context: ({ req }) => ({ req }); without it, GraphQL operations are not profiled.

query: Which collector is ESM-only and what does that imply for wiring it?
expected_behavior: Must read references/collectors.md. Answer: nest-profiler-mikro-orm is the only ESM-only collector ('type': 'module'). It must be imported after MikroOrmModule; it wraps the ORM logger on init and needs no dataSource.

query: I installed the HTTP collector but nothing shows up in the HTTP Client panel. Why?
expected_behavior: Must read references/collectors.md. Answer: the bundled axios adapter is a no-op unless HttpModule (@nestjs/axios) is imported in the SAME module as HttpCollectorModule. For fetch/undici/got, inject HttpProfilerRecorder and call .capture(...) or provide a custom HttpInstrumentation.

query: Can I keep my existing global ValidationPipe when I add the validator collector?
expected_behavior: Must read references/collectors.md. Answer: No — do not register a second global ValidationPipe. The validator collector installs a global ProfilerValidationPipe (APP_PIPE) that wraps your pipe; only one global validator at a time. Fold its options into ValidatorCollectorModule.forRoot({ validationPipeOptions: {...} }) and use value imports (not 'import type') for DTOs.
